title: What Comes to You in the Grave After the Questions

sound.ambient: deep dark ambience, hollow cavernous air, distant low rumble, cold and still, faint cosmic wind, no music, no melody, no instruments

_note: Afterlife Journey Part 2. No recitation = copyright-free. No em dashes.

segments.text: After the angels finish questioning you in the grave… something else arrives. And the Prophet, peace be upon him, said it has a face.
segments.caption: The companion in the grave.

segments.text: For the one who believed, a man appears. Beautiful in face. Sweet in scent. Who are you, the soul asks. And he answers… I am your good deeds.
segments.caption: Sunan Abi Dawud (hadith of al-Bara)

segments.text: Then a window opens, to their place in Paradise. And every morning, and every evening… they are shown the home that is waiting for them.
segments.caption: Sahih al-Bukhari; Muslim

segments.text: But for the one who turned away, the face that comes is dark, and the scent is foul. I am your bad deeds, it says. And a window opens… to the Fire.
segments.caption: Sunan Abi Dawud

segments.text: And there… they wait. Not for years. But until a sound tears through the heavens and the earth.
segments.caption: Until the Trumpet.

segments.sfx: deep low ominous impact, single hollow boom, dark
segments.text: The blast of the Trumpet. And when it comes, everything in the heavens and the earth will fall… except whom Allah wills.
segments.caption: Quran 39:68

segments.text: And that… is where the journey truly begins.
segments.caption: The journey begins.

segments.text: Follow for Part Three… the day the earth gives everyone back.
segments.caption: Follow for Part 3.

segments.text: And if this stirred your heart, let it become a habit. Our app, Ketabi, is a calm home for your faith. Adhkar through your day, a space to reflect, and gentle daily check-ins. Because in the remembrance of Allah… hearts find rest. Begin your journey, at Ketabi Studio dot com.
segments.caption: Download Ketabi.

sources: Sunan Abi Dawud; Musnad Ahmad - the deeds appear as a companion (hadith of al-Bara)
Sahih al-Bukhari 1379; Sahih Muslim 2866 - the window shown morning and evening
Quran 39:68 - the blast of the Trumpet
